channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: US-Iran Live Updates: Kuwait Intercepts Missiles, Drones After New US Strikes On Iran's Qeshm Island

media:content.media:description: US-Iran War Live: The US said that it had "successfully defeated" a series of Iranian missile attacks.

item.description: US-Iran War Live: Iran fired missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain hours after US conducted strikes on Iran's Qeshm Island amid ongoing peace talks between Washington and Tehran.
item.content:encoded: US-Iran War Live: Iran fired missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain hours after US conducted strikes on Iran's Qeshm Island amid ongoing peace talks between Washington and Tehran.

item.title: US Strikes Iran's Qeshm Islands As Tehran Fires Missiles At Kuwait, Bahrain

media:content.media:description: The US said that it conducted self-defense strikes on Iran's Qeshm Island.

item.description: US Strikes Iran's Qeshm Islands As Tehran Fires Missiles At Kuwait, Bahrain
item.content:encoded: US Strikes Iran's Qeshm Islands As Tehran Fires Missiles At Kuwait, Bahrain

item.title: Kuwait Intercepts Missile And Drone Attacks As Middle East Tensions Rise

media:content.media:description: Bahrain's interior ministry also said a warning siren had been sounded.

item.description: Kuwaiti air defences were intercepting hostile missile and drone attacks on Wednesday, state news agency KUNA reported, quoting the country's army which did not say where the threats came from.
item.content:encoded: Kuwaiti air defences were intercepting hostile missile and drone attacks on Wednesday, state news agency KUNA reported, quoting the country's army which did not say where the threats came from.

item.title: Iran Stops Talking To Mediators, Reports Say, But Trump Says Talks Continue

media:content.media:description: Trump called reports of a cessation in talks "false and erroneous."

item.description: Iran stopped communicating with mediators about extending a ceasefire in the war with the US and Israel, two semiofficial Iranian news agencies reported Tuesday.
item.content:encoded: Iran stopped communicating with mediators about extending a ceasefire in the war with the US and Israel, two semiofficial Iranian news agencies reported Tuesday.

item.title: Trump Halts $1.8 Billion 'Anti-Weaponization' Fund To Compensate Supporters
item.description: A federal judge had already temporarily blocked the White House from moving ahead with the program that critics had denounced as a "slush fund" for President Donald Trump's political allies.
item.content:encoded: A federal judge had already temporarily blocked the White House from moving ahead with the program that critics had denounced as a "slush fund" for President Donald Trump's political allies.

item.title: Marco Rubio Says US Considering Naming Official To Run Ebola Response
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio told a House of Representatives committee on Tuesday that the Trump administration is considering appointing a single official to coordinate its response to the Ebola outbreak in Africa.
item.content:encoded: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio told a House of Representatives committee on Tuesday that the Trump administration is considering appointing a single official to coordinate its response to the Ebola outbreak in Africa.

item.title: Trump To Attend New White House Press Gala After Shooting

media:content.media:description: "I was asked to be there, and speak... and have accepted," Trump said on his Truth Social network.

item.description: Trump hailed the announcement by the White House Correspondents Association (WHCA) as a "sign of Strength and Fortitude," in rare praise of the media he often berates.
item.content:encoded: Trump hailed the announcement by the White House Correspondents Association (WHCA) as a "sign of Strength and Fortitude," in rare praise of the media he often berates.

item.title: Pakistan Inaugurates Mosque In Japan. Tokyo Says It's 'Illegal'

media:content.media:description: The embassy called on community members to cooperate with local authorities

item.description: The issue has drawn attention because Pakistan's Ambassador to Japan, Abdul Hameed, attended the mosque's inauguration earlier this year.
item.content:encoded: The issue has drawn attention because Pakistan's Ambassador to Japan, Abdul Hameed, attended the mosque's inauguration earlier this year.

item.title: "As Soon As We Possibly Can": Rubio On Ending Russian Oil Sanction Waivers

media:content.media:description: Rubio said the sanctions waiver was an attempt to alleviate the global effect of the rising oil prices.

item.description: Testifying before the Senate Foreign Policy Committee, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said that the ultimate decision regarding the Russian waiver would be made by the Department of Treasury.
item.content:encoded: Testifying before the Senate Foreign Policy Committee, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said that the ultimate decision regarding the Russian waiver would be made by the Department of Treasury.

item.title: Is Mojtaba Khamenei Alive? Marco Rubio's Big Update On Iran Supreme Leader

media:content.media:description: Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei is alive and has increasingly become active says Rubio

item.description: Khamenei was injured on February 28, the day the United States and Israel launched a joint attack on Iran, which also claimed his father, Ali Khamenei.
item.content:encoded: Khamenei was injured on February 28, the day the United States and Israel launched a joint attack on Iran, which also claimed his father, Ali Khamenei.

item.title: Trump Signs Order Giving Government Access To AI Models Before Release

media:content.media:description: Trump scrapped a Biden-era AI oversight order on his first day back in the White House.

item.description: OpenAI, Google or Anthropic can now give the government access to their most powerful models for up to 30 days before planned release.
item.content:encoded: OpenAI, Google or Anthropic can now give the government access to their most powerful models for up to 30 days before planned release.

item.title: Claude is Offline: Anthropic's AI Chatbot Hits a Global Wall

media:content.media:description: Outage reports reveal that roughly 62% of users are completely locked out

item.description: Hundreds of users are reporting major outages as Anthropic says a fix is already in the works.
item.content:encoded: Hundreds of users are reporting major outages as Anthropic says a fix is already in the works.

item.title: "Dig Of Century": Ancient Coins, Medieval Pottery Unearthed From Notre Dame

media:content.media:description: A slice of Notre Dame's forecourt has become an excavation site.

item.description: At the cathedral's birth in 1163, the entire square was packed with medieval houses, split by a single street.
item.content:encoded: At the cathedral's birth in 1163, the entire square was packed with medieval houses, split by a single street.

item.title: 'Brought Shame On Religion': Sikh Man Jailed For Stabbing UK Teen Over Racism

media:content.media:description: Vickrum Digwa lied to the police about being the victim of a racist attack

item.description: Judge William Mousley of the Southampton Crown Court said that Digwa killed Nowak with a large knife that he kept attached to his belt. Mousley said that Nowak asked the Digwa if he was a "bad man", which led him to feel that he was insulted.
item.content:encoded: Judge William Mousley of the Southampton Crown Court said that Digwa killed Nowak with a large knife that he kept attached to his belt. Mousley said that Nowak asked the Digwa if he was a "bad man", which led him to feel that he was insulted.

item.title: UK Now Using Elon Musk's Militarised Satellite Network Starshield: Report

media:content.media:description: The British military began using Starlink in July 2022.

item.description: Britain's defence ministry started transitioning operational military traffic to the more expensive Starshield service around the beginning of this year.
item.content:encoded: Britain's defence ministry started transitioning operational military traffic to the more expensive Starshield service around the beginning of this year.

item.title: Hackers Trick Meta's AI To Infiltrate Instagram Accounts, Including Obama-Era White House Archive

media:content.media:description: Meta spokesperson Andy Stone confirmed that the vulnerability has since been fixed.

item.description: Among the accounts reportedly compromised were the Obama-era White House archive account, beauty retailer Sephora, US Space Force Chief Master Sergeant John Bentivegna, and cybersecurity researcher Jane Manchun Wong.
item.content:encoded: Among the accounts reportedly compromised were the Obama-era White House archive account, beauty retailer Sephora, US Space Force Chief Master Sergeant John Bentivegna, and cybersecurity researcher Jane Manchun Wong.

item.title: The $15 Billion 'Floating City': Inside The Vision For The World's Largest Ship. See Pics

media:content.media:description: The vessel would continuously travel around the globe.

item.description: If built, the vessel would be unlike anything ever seen in maritime history, carrying up to 80,000 people and dwarfing even today's largest cruise ships.
item.content:encoded: If built, the vessel would be unlike anything ever seen in maritime history, carrying up to 80,000 people and dwarfing even today's largest cruise ships.

item.title: Climate Change Will Make Super El Nino Especially Severe, Scientists Warn

media:content.media:description: WMO forecasts suggest a strong El Nino is possible.

item.description: The World Meteorological Organisation said there is an 80 per cent chance that an El Nino event develops between June and August.
item.content:encoded: The World Meteorological Organisation said there is an 80 per cent chance that an El Nino event develops between June and August.

item.title: Kremlin Accuses Ukraine Of Committing "Acts Of Terror" Against Civilians

media:content.media:description: Moscow has said it is stepping up its strikes on military targets in Ukraine.

item.description: Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov made the remarks to reporters when asked about Russia's massive overnight strikes on Ukrainian cities, which killed at least 17 people and wounded 100.
item.content:encoded: Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov made the remarks to reporters when asked about Russia's massive overnight strikes on Ukrainian cities, which killed at least 17 people and wounded 100.

item.title: US-Iran War Live Updates: Iranian Officer Says Renewed War With US 'Inevitable' Amid Trump's Peace Push
item.description: US-Iran war live updates: The remark by Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf came as reports suggested US President Donald Trump has sent back a revised, tougher peace proposal
item.content:encoded: US-Iran war live updates: The remark by Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf came as reports suggested US President Donald Trump has sent back a revised, tougher peace proposal
